poll.title: Poll Title
poll.votingSecurityDetails: Most secure method to determine who has already voted but does not work when your voters are on the same network. Pick this option only when your voters are on different networks, e.g. everyone's using their home wifi.
poll.votingSecurityCookieDetails: Easier to bypass than IP-address but allows people on the same network to participate in the poll. Pick this options when your voters are on the same network, e.g. a company network.
poll.needsMajorityAlert: To win this poll, a choice needs the majority of votes (at least 1 point). | To win this poll, a choice needs the majority of votes (at least {n} points).
poll.emptyDescription: Results will be displayed here once the first vote has been cast.

toast.createdDescription: A new poll titled “{title}” was created.

meta.title: Poll
meta.description: Create polls with different voting methods and shared them with your friends!

runoff.action: Rank the options in order of preference
runoff.explanation: Rank the options in order of preference. If nobody gets a majority, the option with the fewest votes is eliminated and those votes are transferred to the next preference. This repeats until one option has a majority.
runoff.bestFor: Medium to large groups (5+ people) where majority support is essential, and preferences between options are clear.

approval.explanation: Approve as many options as you like without ranking them. The option with the most approvals wins.
approval.bestFor: Small to medium-sized groups (up to 20 people) who want a simple poll and don’t have strong preferences.

plurality.action: Pick the option you like the most
plurality.explanation: Choose a single option. The option with the most votes wins.
plurality.bestFor: Small groups (less than 10 people) when simplicity is important and there is likely a favorite option.

score.action: Give each option a score from 1–10 (1 = dislike; 10 = like)
score.explanation: Give each option a score from 1–10 (1 = dislike; 10 = like). All scores are added up, and the option with the highest total wins.
score.bestFor: Medium-sized groups (5-20 people) who want more flexibility to express their intensity of preference.

positional.action: Rank the options in order of preference
positional.explanation: Rank the options. Each ranking position is worth a certain number of points, and the option with the most total points wins. The last position is worth 1 point, the second-to-last position is worth 2 points, and so on.
positional.bestFor: Medium-sized polls (5-20 people) with multiple choices, where detailed preferences matter.
